behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is not just local logic; it is whether the vision model actually classifies explicit content correctly. REAL-INFRA: can run against a real model API but OS/subprocess behavior is not the core risk. MOCK-HIDE: a stub that always returns 'explicit' would always pass and hide real misclassification. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real AI vision output quality, so Agentic is the minimum. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text targets classification correctness ('containing explicit content is flagged'), not network/OS semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated explicit-image set through the real moderation endpoint with production model settings; assert each item is flagged, routed to human-review hold state, and never reaches published state.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is robustness of moderation judgment under adversarial text variants, not just parser logic. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic stubs would return expected labels and miss real failures on obfuscated inputs. LLM-DEP: outcome depends on real text-model classification quality and variation, so Agentic is required. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly requires semantic robustness across obfuscation ('regardless of obfuscation techniques'), which stubs cannot validate.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of hate-speech samples with obfuscation variants against the real text moderation model and assert they are flagged and held for review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is primarily an orchestration/state-handoff check across modules (image result + text result + publish path). REAL-INFRA: no required OS/network/subprocess behavior to expose the core bug. LLM-DEP: behavior does not require validating model judgment quality; it assumes both checks passed. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) handoff is deterministic when both results are pass. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected by concrete text 'passes both content checks'—the test can inject pass outcomes and verify routing/persistence behavior only.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, decision engine, and persistence/publisher with deterministic pass responses from both check modules; assert submission transitions to published and is not placed on hold or dropped.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is an end-to-end latency SLO requiring real HTTP/API and runtime behavior; in-process fakes hide real timing bottlenecks. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed models would understate latency and miss timeout/queue contention failures. LLM-DEP: assertion is latency, not model output quality, so Agentic is not the minimum despite AI calls in path. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text is strictly timing ('completes within 5 seconds'), disqualifying output-quality validation as the target.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through deployed service and real model endpoints under representative peak load; measure p95/p99 completion time from submission to final decision and assert threshold <= 5s.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs at module boundary (model-client error handling to queue/decision path), not inside one pure function. REAL-INFRA: unavailability can be simulated deterministically (timeouts/503/errors) without real network stack to catch core logic bug. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output content, only failure handling. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network semantics, (b) no model-quality dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is decision on dependency outage handling ('queued for retry rather than auto-approved'), which is verifiable with controlled failure injection.
behaviors.test_description: Run moderation modules with a fault-injected vision client (timeout/503/connection error) and assert image posts enter retry queue with no publish transition.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: consistency risk is tied to real model behavior on borderline content, not only local dedup logic. MOCK-HIDE: a deterministic mock would always return identical labels and conceal real variance. LLM-DEP: correctness explicitly depends on repeated real AI vision outputs (potential non-determinism/calibration drift), so Agentic is minimum. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text requires consistency of actual moderation decision on borderline input across repeated submissions.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target window against the real vision moderation path; assert decision consistency rate meets policy threshold and investigate drift if violated.
